name: Notion MCP
description: Connect Claude to Notion's official MCP server to read and write pages, query databases, search the workspace, and manage comments.
author: Notion
skill_content:
---
name: Notion MCP
description: Connect Claude to Notion (official MCP) — read/write pages, query databases, search, and manage comments.
---

# Notion MCP

Notion's **official** Model Context Protocol server. It lets Claude read and edit page content as Markdown, query and update databases, search across the workspace, and read/write comments — about 22 tools in total.

## Connect (hosted, recommended)

Notion offers a hosted remote server that authenticates via OAuth — no local install. See Notion's setup docs (developers.notion.com/docs/mcp) for the current endpoint and one-click install.

## Connect (local, self-managed)

Run the published package with a Notion integration token:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "notion": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@notionhq/notion-mcp-server"],
      "env": { "NOTION_TOKEN": "ntn_****" }
    }
  }
}
```

Create the integration token in Notion **Settings → Connections → Develop or manage integrations**, then share the pages/databases you want Claude to access with that integration.

## Pairs well with

- **Notion Database Designer** — design the schema, then let this connector build and populate it.

Source & license: https://github.com/makenotion/notion-mcp-server (MIT).

name: Jira & Confluence MCP (Atlassian)
description: Connect Claude to Atlassian's official remote MCP server for Jira issues and Confluence pages via OAuth.
author: Atlassian
skill_content:
---
name: Jira & Confluence MCP (Atlassian)
description: Connect Claude to Atlassian's official remote MCP for Jira issues and Confluence pages.
---

# Jira & Confluence MCP (Atlassian)

Atlassian's **official remote** MCP server ("Rovo"). It securely connects Jira and Confluence to Claude so you can search and read issues, create and update them, comment, and work with Confluence pages — all scoped to your Atlassian permissions.

## Connect (hosted remote, OAuth)

The server is hosted by Atlassian at `https://mcp.atlassian.com/v1/sse` and authenticates with your Atlassian account via OAuth — no local install or API token to manage. Follow Atlassian's one-click setup for Claude (support.atlassian.com → Atlassian Remote MCP Server → Getting started).

## Notes

- Cloud only. For self-hosted Jira/Confluence Data Center, use a community MCP that targets the Data Center REST API instead.
- Access is bounded by the signed-in user's existing Jira/Confluence permissions.

## Pairs well with

- **Jira Ticket Writer** — draft well-formed issues, then file them through this connector.

Source & license: https://github.com/atlassian/atlassian-mcp-server (Apache-2.0).

name: Linear MCP
description: Connect Claude to Linear's official hosted MCP server to manage issues, projects, cycles, and teams via OAuth.
author: Linear
skill_content:
---
name: Linear MCP
description: Connect Claude to Linear's official hosted MCP — manage issues, projects, cycles, and teams.
---

# Linear MCP

Linear's **official hosted** MCP server. It lets Claude create and update issues, move them through workflow states, manage projects and cycles, and read team and label structure.

## Connect (hosted remote, OAuth)

The server is hosted by Linear at `https://mcp.linear.app/mcp` and authenticates via OAuth — no local install and no API key to store. See Linear's MCP docs for the current one-click setup for Claude.

## Notes

- Access mirrors your Linear permissions and team membership.
- Reads and writes happen live against your workspace — review changes before bulk operations.

## Pairs well with

- **Linear Workflow** — set up cycles, labels, SLAs, and triage, then operate the workspace through this connector.

Source & docs: https://linear.app/docs/mcp (hosted service; see Linear's terms).

name: Asana MCP
description: Connect Claude to Asana's official MCP server to manage tasks, projects, workspaces, dependencies, and comments via OAuth.
author: Asana
skill_content:
---
name: Asana MCP
description: Connect Claude to Asana's official MCP — manage tasks, projects, workspaces, dependencies, and comments.
---

# Asana MCP

Asana's **official** MCP server. It lets Claude create and update tasks, search and organize projects, manage subtasks and dependencies, and read/write comments across your workspaces.

## Connect (hosted remote, OAuth)

The server is hosted by Asana at `https://mcp.asana.com/sse` (a v2 endpoint, `https://mcp.asana.com/v2/mcp`, is also available) and authenticates via OAuth — no local install. See Asana's developer docs for the current setup for Claude.

## Notes

- Access is scoped to the signed-in user's Asana workspaces and permissions.
- Confirm the target project/workspace before batch-creating or moving tasks.

Source & docs: https://developers.asana.com/docs/using-asanas-mcp-server (official Asana service).

# ClickUp MCP

ClickUp's **official** MCP server (an expanding tool set covering tasks, lists, docs, time tracking, and workspace structure).

## Connect (hosted remote, OAuth)

The server is hosted by ClickUp at `https://mcp.clickup.com/mcp` and authenticates via OAuth — no local install or token to store. Follow ClickUp's setup guide (developer.clickup.com → Connect an AI assistant to ClickUp's MCP server).

## Notes

- Prefer this official server over community ClickUp MCPs — several of those ship without a clear license.
- Access is bounded by your ClickUp workspace permissions.

Source & docs: https://developer.clickup.com/docs/connect-an-ai-assistant-to-clickups-mcp-server-1 (official ClickUp service).

name: monday.com MCP
description: Connect Claude to monday.com's official MCP server to manage boards, items, and workflows via OAuth.
author: monday.com
skill_content:
---
name: monday.com MCP
description: Connect Claude to monday.com's official MCP — manage boards, items, and workflows.
---

# monday.com MCP

monday.com's **official** MCP servers. The **Platform MCP** gives Claude secure access to boards and items (create/update items, read columns, manage workflows); a separate **Apps MCP** helps build and deploy monday apps.

## Connect (hosted Platform MCP, OAuth)

The hosted Platform MCP requires no local setup — add the server and authenticate via OAuth when prompted. See monday's developer docs (developer.monday.com → MCP servers) and monday.com/w/mcp.

## Connect (local, self-managed)

The official package `@mondaydotcomorg/monday-api-mcp` provides a plug-and-play server that authenticates with your existing monday API token.

## Notes

- Access is scoped to the authenticated user's monday account and board permissions.

Source & license: https://github.com/mondaycom/mcp (official monday.com).

name: Airtable MCP
description: Connect Claude to Airtable bases to read/write records, search, and inspect or edit schema. Community server (no official Airtable MCP).
author: domdomegg
skill_content:
---
name: Airtable MCP
description: Connect Claude to Airtable — read/write records, search, and inspect or edit schema. Community MCP server.
---

# Airtable MCP

A well-maintained **community** MCP server for Airtable (`domdomegg/airtable-mcp-server`). Airtable does not publish an official MCP, so this is the de-facto standard. It lets Claude list bases and tables, describe schema, read/search records, and (with write scopes) create and update records, tables, and fields.

## Connect (local, npx)

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "airtable": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "airtable-mcp-server"],
      "env": { "AIRTABLE_API_KEY": "pat123.abc123" }
    }
  }
}
```

Create a **personal access token** in Airtable with the scopes you need: `schema.bases:read` and `data.records:read` for read-only; add `schema.bases:write`, `data.records:write`, and the comment scopes for writes.

## Tools

Read: `list_bases`, `list_tables`, `describe_table`, `list_records`, `search_records`, `get_record`, `list_comments`. Write: `create_record`, `update_records`, `delete_records`, `create_table`, `update_table`, `create_field`, `update_field`, `create_comment`.

## Notes

- Third-party (not built by Airtable). Review the source before connecting it to sensitive bases.

Source & license: https://github.com/domdomegg/airtable-mcp-server (MIT).
